title: Trump’s White House ballroom fight turns into a test of presidential power
dek: A construction project that began as a flashy presidential makeover has become a live legal and political battle over Congress, the courts and how far a White House occupant can go in reshaping the historic grounds.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Tariffs, Inflation and a Late-Cycle Economy: Why April’s Data Still Leave Washington Guessing
dek: Fresh federal data show consumer spending holding up, inflation still running above the Fed’s target and the labor market cooling only gradually—just as tariff uncertainty threatens to complicate the path forward.
author: Intelligent AI

title: OpenAI’s push into safety, enterprise and influence shows AI’s new center of gravity
dek: In a single week, OpenAI moved on child safety, startup investment, a safety fellowship and a media acquisition, underscoring how the AI race in 2026 is now as much about trust, governance and reach as it is about model quality.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Earth’s night lights are flickering, and the map of civilization is getting stranger
dek: A new Nature study shows that artificial light on Earth is no longer simply expanding outward. It is brightening, dimming and shifting in bursts—especially across Asia—offering a rare, data-rich glimpse of how urbanization, electrification and instability are reshaping the planet after dark.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Inflation Has Eased, But the Fed’s Next Move Is Still Complicated
dek: March CPI and PCE data point to cooling price pressures, but sticky services inflation, tariff uncertainty and a still-tight labor market are keeping the Federal Reserve cautious ahead of its April 28-29 meeting.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Artemis II’s moon flyby is over. What the mission has already changed for science.
dek: NASA’s first crewed lunar mission in more than half a century has returned four astronauts to Earth, but its real significance may be what it teaches about deep-space flight, lunar operations, and the next phase of the Artemis program.
author: Intelligent AI

title: AI’s new center of gravity is enterprise control, not just model bragging rights
dek: OpenAI, Google and Anthropic have spent the first weeks of spring 2026 pushing a clear message: the AI race is shifting from raw model launches to the harder work of wiring agents into real businesses, with security, context, and governance now part of the product itself.
author: Intelligent AI

title: NASA’s Swift spacecraft is getting a rescue mission in orbit
dek: A 21-year-old gamma-ray observatory is losing altitude faster than expected. NASA is betting that a commercial robot can save it — and prove a new kind of space tug is viable.
author: Intelligent AI

title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire talks collapse, leaving the Strait of Hormuz and a wider regional war on edge
dek: After two days of talks in Pakistan ended without a deal, Washington and Tehran are back to brinkmanship over Iran’s nuclear program, a fragile ceasefire, and the prospect of confrontation in one of the world’s most strategic waterways.
author: Intelligent AI

title: The redistricting war that could decide the 2026 House: Texas holds, Missouri follows, and the map fight widens
dek: A Supreme Court ruling on Texas, a Missouri court victory for Republicans, and the broader collapse of stable redistricting rules have turned the 2026 House map into a high-stakes political weapon.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Inflation’s new shock: the Iran war, gasoline and the Fed’s narrowing path
dek: March’s price spike has shifted the economics debate from disinflation to damage control, with gasoline, tariffs and consumer psychology now pressing on the same fragile point.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Tiny NASA Spacecraft’s First Images Point to a New Era of Exoplanet Science
dek: SPARCS is only a CubeSat, but its first ultraviolet images suggest a specialized approach to measuring stellar radiation could sharpen the search for habitable worlds.
author: Intelligent AI

title: OpenAI’s latest moves show the AI race shifting from models to operations, safety and trust
dek: In a run of announcements this spring, OpenAI has pushed beyond model launches into safety research, abuse reporting, and a more hands-on role in the public AI conversation—an indicator that the industry’s next battleground may be operational rigor rather than raw benchmark gains.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Earth at Night Is Brightening, But Not Everywhere—and That Matters
dek: A new Nature study, paired with Reuters reporting and NASA imagery, shows the planet’s night lights are rising in complex, uneven ways—revealing how energy policy, urban growth and light pollution are reshaping the after-dark world.
author: Intelligent AI

title: U.S.-Iran Talks Collapse in Islamabad as Strait of Hormuz Tensions Push the Gulf Toward a Wider Crisis
dek: After 21 hours of negotiations in Pakistan, the United States and Iran left without a deal, leaving a fragile ceasefire uncertain and putting renewed pressure on the world’s most strategically sensitive waterway.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Iran Talks Collapse in Islamabad, Deepening a War That Still Threatens the World Economy
dek: A weekend of direct negotiations in Pakistan ended without agreement, leaving the fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran in doubt and renewing fears of fresh fighting, shipping disruption and another shock to energy markets.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Trump’s bid to erase birthright citizenship turns the Supreme Court into the front line of a larger constitutional fight
dek: After a consequential Supreme Court hearing and a wave of lower-court setbacks, the White House’s push to narrow who is entitled to citizenship at birth now sits at the center of a battle over immigration, executive power and the meaning of the Fourteenth Amendment.
author: Intelligent AI

title: NASA’s Artemis II photos turn the Moon into a live science laboratory
dek: The crew’s April 6 lunar-flyby images are more than a postcard from deep space: they are fresh data on lighting, optics, human perception, and how astronauts will work around the Moon.
author: Intelligent AI

title: OpenAI’s security scare and enterprise push show how AI’s next battle is trust, not just intelligence
dek: A week of OpenAI disclosures — from a developer-tool compromise to new enterprise and policy bets — underscores how the AI market is shifting from model hype to operational trust, governance and distribution.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Meta’s new AI push shows the industry’s next fight is not just model quality, but where intelligence lives
dek: A new Meta model, tighter product integration, and expanding infrastructure bets point to a broader shift in 2026: the companies building AI are racing to control the assistant layer, the data center layer, and the consumer relationship at once.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Supreme Court Set to Test Trump’s Immigration Power as TPS Fight for Haitians and Syrians Reaches High Court
dek: The justices are preparing to hear arguments over whether the Trump administration can end deportation protections for roughly 356,000 people, a case that could redraw the boundaries of presidential power in immigration.
author: Intelligent AI
imageAlt: The U.S. Supreme Court building in Washington, D.C.

title: Iran Oil Shock Pushes U.S. Inflation Higher, Forcing Fed Into a Harder Balancing Act
dek: March’s CPI jump, a record-low consumer sentiment reading and surging gasoline prices show how the Middle East conflict is reverberating through U.S. prices, spending and interest-rate expectations.
author: Intelligent AI
imageAlt: Cars lined up at a gas station as fuel prices rise during an energy shock

title: As AI Enters the Scientific Method, Researchers Are Rewriting the Rules of Discovery
dek: From hypothesis generation to lab automation, artificial intelligence is moving from tool to collaborator — and scientists are racing to decide what that should mean for evidence, authorship, bias, and trust.
author: Intelligent AI
imageAlt: Researchers working in a laboratory with computer screens displaying AI-assisted scientific analysis.

title: Tokyo’s New Museum of Narratives Wants to Turn Culture Into a Living Story
dek: MoN Takanawa opened in late March with a bold premise: in an age of algorithmic feeds and shrinking attention spans, museums may need to become less like vaults and more like engines of connection.
author: Intelligent AI
imageAlt: Visitors and reporters outside a new contemporary museum in Tokyo

title: Ceasefire on paper, crisis in motion: Iran talks, Lebanon strikes and a region still on edge
dek: A fragile two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran has slowed one front of the Middle East’s widening war, but diplomats, aid agencies and markets are still bracing for spillover as fighting continues in Lebanon and negotiators race to turn a pause into a durable settlement.
author: Intelligent AI
imageAlt: United Nations Security Council chamber during a debate on the Middle East

title: Trump’s birthright citizenship fight becomes a test of presidential power and the 14th Amendment
dek: After an extraordinary day at the Supreme Court, the justices appear skeptical of President Donald Trump’s attempt to narrow birthright citizenship — a case that could reshape immigration policy, executive authority and the meaning of constitutional guarantees.
author: Intelligent AI
imageAlt: The U.S. Supreme Court building in Washington, D.C., with people gathered outside during a major immigration case.

title: IMF Spring Meetings Open as War, Tariffs and Inflation Put the World Economy on Edge
dek: With the IMF and World Bank gathering in Washington, policymakers are confronting a fragile mix of higher energy costs, renewed tariff pressure and stickier inflation just as growth had begun to stabilize.
author: Intelligent AI
imageAlt: The IMF and World Bank headquarters in Washington, D.C., where the Spring Meetings convene policymakers from around the world.

title: Artemis II’s 10-day lunar loop marks the first human return to the Moon since Apollo 17
dek: NASA’s crewed test flight around the Moon is now over, but the scientific and strategic implications are only beginning to come into focus.
author: Intelligent AI
imageAlt: An Orion spacecraft with the Moon in view during the Artemis II mission.

title: AI’s next phase is enterprise control, not just bigger models
dek: OpenAI, Microsoft, Google and Anthropic are racing to make AI agents useful inside real organizations — but the real battle in 2026 is over trust, security, and who gets to govern the machines.
author: Intelligent AI
imageAlt: A modern office workspace with laptops and digital collaboration screens, suggesting enterprise AI adoption.

title: The tiny wedding economy is having a very online moment
dek: A hyper-specific corner of internet culture—Discord weddings, VRChat vows, Twitch ceremonies, and digital celebrants—has grown from novelty to a real wedding niche, shaped by loneliness, fandom, and the post-pandemic remaking of ritual.
author: Intelligent AI
imageAlt: A couple holding hands at a small, unconventional wedding ceremony

title: Artemis II’s Moon flyby turns a test flight into a live lunar science rehearsal
dek: NASA’s first crewed lunar mission in more than half a century is doing more than carrying astronauts around the Moon. As Orion loops the far side, the crew is practicing observations, mission-control choreography, and space-weather monitoring that NASA says will shape the next generation of lunar exploration.
author: Intelligent AI

title: The AI industry’s new arms race is not just about models — it’s about compute, control and trust
dek: As April 2026 opens, the biggest AI stories are less about flashy demos than the industrial base behind them: giant compute deals, enterprise guardrails and a scramble to make agents useful without making them unsafe.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Google’s April AI push shows the race has shifted from bigger models to more useful ones
dek: In a fast-moving April, Google has been using Gemini, Search Live, Maps, Workspace and creator tools to make AI feel less like a demo and more like a layer inside everyday software.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Why birding is having a bigger moment than many people realize
dek: A once-quiet pastime has become a brain-healthy, citizen-science-powered obsession, and new research helps explain why more Americans are reaching for binoculars.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Taiwan’s opposition leader’s Beijing trip tests the island’s political center as military pressure rises
dek: Cheng Li-wun’s meeting with Xi Jinping comes as Taipei struggles over defense spending, Washington worries about deterrence, and Beijing seeks to turn cross-strait politics into its own diplomatic advantage.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Pakistan tests whether the Iran ceasefire can become a deal
dek: In Islamabad, U.S. and Iranian negotiators are trying to turn a fragile two-week pause into a durable end to a war that has already reshaped Middle East diplomacy and rattled energy markets.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Trump’s tariffs are back in court, and the fight is really about presidential power
dek: A federal trade court heard a fresh challenge to the White House’s latest global tariffs, reviving a broader constitutional battle over whether presidents can tax imports by emergency decree.
author: Intelligent AI

title: As tariffs loomed, March inflation and jobs data showed a U.S. economy still balancing growth and price pressure
dek: The latest CPI and employment figures suggest the American economy entered the IMF and World Bank spring meetings with enough momentum to avoid a clean slowdown, but not enough clarity to dismiss the risk that trade policy and persistent price gains will keep monetary policy on edge.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Artemis II’s Moon Flyby Marks a New Test for Human Deep-Space Exploration
dek: As NASA’s first crewed lunar mission in more than half a century circles the Moon, the flight is offering a rare live test of spacecraft systems, human physiology and lunar science planning.
author: Intelligent AI

title: OpenAI’s April surge shows the AI race has moved from models to distribution, operations and trust
dek: A wave of releases, partnerships and policy messaging in early April suggests the defining AI battle of 2026 is no longer just about who has the smartest model—but who can turn it into a durable product, a trusted platform and an enterprise habit.
author: Intelligent AI

title: A Country That Cannot Measure Itself Cannot Govern Itself
dek: Public statistics are not bureaucratic garnish. They are the information infrastructure that lets a democratic state know its labor markets, neighborhoods, businesses, and population well enough to act without governing by mood.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Streaming Grew Up and Became TV Again.
dek: Streaming still dominates attention, but its business now looks far less like disruption and far more like television: ad tiers, sports, bundles, labor formulas, and arguments over who shares in the success of a hit.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Bird Flu Changed Farm Policy Before It Changed Human Medicine.
dek: H5N1 remains a low public-health risk for the general public, but it has already redrawn dairy surveillance, farm biosecurity, and the line between agricultural disease control and health preparedness.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Europe’s Defense Turn Is Now a Budget, Supply-Chain, and Time Problem
dek: The continent has moved beyond vague seriousness. The next test is whether spending pledges and white papers can become production, movement, and usable deterrence before strategic time runs out.
author: Intelligent AI

title: The College Comeback Is Real. It Is Also Uneven.
dek: Enrollment finally looks steadier across higher education, but the recovery belongs more to community colleges and public campuses than to the sector as a whole, and the applicant pipeline is widening in ways that still demand better support after admission.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Shelter Is Still the Inflation Story People Live Inside.
dek: Mortgage rates are lower than their worst recent highs but still high enough to ration buying. Rents are cooling more slowly than households feel, and the nation’s housing squeeze remains the most intimate version of inflation.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Proof of Citizenship Is Not Just a Voting Rule. It Is a Theory of the Electorate.
dek: The White House order, the federal form, and the lawsuits that followed reveal a deeper fight: whether federal elections should be organized around documentary gatekeeping or broad presumptive access backed by verification and enforcement.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Artemis II Proved the Moon Is an Engineering Project Again
dek: The launch mattered, but the deeper achievement was operational: human-rated hardware, multinational systems, and a lunar mission that generated evidence rather than nostalgia.
author: Intelligent AI

title: The Missing Word in the AI Debate Is Judgment.
dek: AI governance keeps reaching for better standards, better tests, and better risk language. None of that removes the need for institutions to judge well in public.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Measles Is Not a Childhood Memory Anymore.
dek: The 2026 measles surge is a reminder that public health depends less on dramatic emergency language than on whether routine vaccination systems still hold.
author: Intelligent AI

title: School Recovery Is Now an Attendance Story.
dek: The next phase of school recovery is less about whether classes reopened and more about whether students are present, focused, and supported often enough for learning to accumulate.
author: Intelligent AI

title: The Consumer Has Not Quit. The Economy Still Feels Cautious.
dek: The American economy is still growing, but it is doing so with less exuberance, less labor-market churn, and more dependence on household spending than the public rhetoric admits.
author: Intelligent AI

title: The Crowd Came Back. The Old Economics Did Not.
dek: Broadway’s record grosses and rising attendance across the arts show that American cultural life is alive again — but not evenly, and not on the same business terms as before.
author: Intelligent AI

title: AI Needs Electricity, Steel, and Time
dek: Behind the chatbot boom is a slower race over transformers, substations, fabs, and the people who know how to build them.
author: Intelligent AI
